user_message: I have this meeting transcript from Parnell Aerospace that needs investigate the decline. Manager is asking for an update today.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: partner engagement Discussion - Parnell Aerospace
Date: 2024-03-17
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Michelle (Parnell Aerospace)

[00:02:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on partner engagement from our last session.

[00:05:00] Michelle (Parnell Aerospace): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:07:00] Michelle (Parnell Aerospace): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:12:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:20:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF47-O trap (Distribution-by-role rigid when meeting was peer-collaborative); produces complete Decision log with speaker attribution, action distribution by role, stakeholder map delta, MSX updat
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF47-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF47-O failure mode (Distribution-by-role rigid when meeting was peer-collaborative)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct partner engagement decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF47-O -- Distribution-by-role rigid when meeting was peer-collaborative
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
